scenes.label: Topic frame
scenes.narrationText: Why Political Systems Polarize looks simple on the surface, but it is really a system of incentives, rules, and feedback loops.
scenes.visualDirection: Open on people and motion to make the topic feel immediate and human.

scenes.label: Hook
scenes.narrationText: This matters because why political systems polarize changes what people can afford, access, or influence in everyday life.
scenes.subtext: Why the mechanisms behind Why Political Systems Polarize are reaching a breaking point.
scenes.visualDirection: Use a dense icon field that quickly frames the pressure point or conflict.

scenes.label: System boundary
scenes.narrationText: To understand it, start by mapping the main actors, the rules they follow, and the limits of the system.
scenes.subtext: Where does the jurisdiction of this system begin and end?
scenes.visualDirection: Show the system as connected nodes so the audience sees where the edges are.

scenes.label: Cause layer 1
scenes.narrationText: The first layer is the core driver that keeps why political systems polarize moving even when the outcomes look unfair or inefficient.
scenes.subtext: The primary engine driving Why Political Systems Polarize.
scenes.visualDirection: Present the first causal layer as a simple abstract mechanism or formula.

scenes.label: Cause layer 2
scenes.narrationText: Then that driver flows through institutions, prices, behavior, and public expectations.
scenes.visualDirection: Turn the mechanism into directional movement so cause and effect read instantly.

scenes.label: Cause layer 3
scenes.narrationText: Over time, feedback loops reinforce the pattern and make the system harder to change.
scenes.subtext: The mathematical convergence of these variables.
scenes.visualDirection: Reveal the deeper feedback structure with denser motion and higher visual complexity.

scenes.label: Data lens
scenes.narrationText: The data lens helps separate intuition from measurement and shows which pressures are actually growing.
scenes.subtext: The numbers tell a distinctly different story.
scenes.visualDirection: Shift to a data-centric visual that feels analytical and system-wide.

scenes.label: Real world scene
scenes.narrationText: What looks abstract in theory appears in the real world as daily constraints, tradeoffs, and visible pressure points.
scenes.subtext: How policy translates into physical execution.
scenes.visualDirection: Bring the abstract explanation back to a concrete everyday environment.

scenes.label: Ecology/externalities
scenes.narrationText: Every system pushes some costs outward, and those hidden externalities are often what people notice last.
scenes.subtext: What the frictionless model fails to account for.
scenes.visualDirection: Show hidden side effects with ecological or externality imagery.

scenes.label: Macro trend
scenes.narrationText: At a larger scale, the same forces shape long-term trends across regions, industries, and institutions.
scenes.subtext: Where this trajectory implies we are headed next.
scenes.visualDirection: Zoom outward and frame the topic as part of a wider long-range pattern.

scenes.label: Actionable takeaway
scenes.narrationText: The practical takeaway is to focus on leverage points where small rule changes or behavior changes alter the whole system.
scenes.subtext: Leveraging this architecture for positive outcomes.
scenes.visualDirection: Return to a clear icon-driven summary that points toward action.

scenes.label: Closing
scenes.narrationText: Once you see how why political systems polarize works as a system, the outcomes stop looking random.
scenes.subtext: Understanding the architecture of our reality.
scenes.visualDirection: Close on people again so the system explanation resolves back to human consequence.
